info.title: Reality Management
info.description:
Interact with your reality data.

View, update, create, and delete reality data.

get.description:
---

Retrieves a list of [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata) instances belonging to the specified iTwin.

The retrieved instances are those for which you have the required access rights, relative to the specified iTwin.

The `iTwinId` is optional. If you don't provide it, the retrieved [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata) instances will be relative to general access rights provided by your organization. These rights are usually more restrictive, so we recommend using the `iTwinId` parameter for exhaustive results.

### Notes

The `Prefer` header can be used to specify how much result metadata is desired by the client. The `Prefer` request header field is used to indicate that particular server behaviors are preferred by the client but are not required for successful completion of the request.

This operation supports `"return=representation"` and `"return=minimal"` preferences.

The `"return=representation"` preference indicates that the client prefers that the server include an entity representing the current state of the resource in the response to a successful request, i.e., all properties are included in the response.
The `"return=minimal"` preference indicates that the client wishes the server to return only a minimal response to a successful request. This is the default preference if `Prefer` header is not specified.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin or must have access to context.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

parameters.name: iTwinId
parameters.description: Id of iTwin. The operation gets all reality data in this iTwin.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Parameter that enables continuing to the next page of the previous paged query. This must be passed exactly as it is in the response body's _links.next property. If this is specified and $top is omitted, the next page will be the same size as the previous page.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: The number of reality data to get in each page. Max 1000, but 100 is the default if this parameter is not included.

parameters.name: extent
parameters.description: Extent of the area to search, delimited by southwest and northeast coordinates. Extent values are specified in this format: SouthwestCorner_Longitude, SouthwestCorner_Latitude, NortheastCorner_Longitude, NortheastCorner_Latitude. E.g. to get all reality data in an area around Exton, PA, provide the following extent parameter: extent=-75.637679,40.032871,-75.633647,40.032771

parameters.name: $orderBy
parameters.description: Parameter that enable to order reality data in ascending or descending order. Default is ascending.Example : displayName desc

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Comma separated list of reality data types. Example: "OPC,Terrain3DTiles, OMR, Cesium3DTiles"

parameters.name: acquisitionDateTime
parameters.description:
Aquisition datetime range in ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC).
Format: startDateTime/endDateTime
Example: 2021-05-12T20:03:12Z/2022-05-12T20:03:12Z

parameters.name: createdDateTime
parameters.description:
Created datetime range in ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC).
Format: startDateTime/endDateTime
Example: 2021-05-12T20:03:12Z/2022-05-12T20:03:12Z

parameters.name: modifiedDateTime
parameters.description:
Modified datetime range in ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC).
Format: startDateTime/endDateTime
Example: 2021-05-12T20:03:12Z/2022-05-12T20:03:12Z

parameters.name: lastAccessedDateTime
parameters.description:
Last accessed datetime range in ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC).
Format: startDateTime/endDateTime
Example: 2021-05-12T20:03:12Z/2022-05-12T20:03:12Z

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: Guid identifier of the owner. Example: 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Parameter to get reality data with exact matching tags.

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

403.description: This response indicates that user does not have required permissions to get  reality data's from iTwin.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

404.description: This response indicates that the iTwin was not found.

error.message: Requested iTwin is not available.

422.description: Invalid request to get reality data. Please ensure that the request is valid.

error.message: Invalid RealityData request.

details.message: The field Top must be between 1 and 1000.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

post.description:
---
Creates a [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata).

As shown in the request section below, you must provide a request body.

You MUST provide the `iTwinId`, `displayName` and `type` properties.

The `rootDocument` property is not required but highly recommended. It is the entry point for consuming applications to read the content of the Reality Data. [See RootDocument property](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/reality-management/rm-rd-details/#rootdocument)

For more details on each property, see [Reality Data Properties](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/reality-management/rm-rd-details/) in documentation.

After creation, the reality data will be associated to the specified iTwin.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `realitydata_create` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.
---

example.displayName: Name of the reality data to create
example.termsOfUse: Terms of use of reality data

realityData.termsOfUse: Terms of use of reality data

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

422.description: Invalid request to create reality data. Please ensure that the request is valid.

error.message: Invalid request to create reality data. Can not create reality data.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

patch.description:
---

Updates the metadata of a [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata).

The `iTwinId` parameter is optional, but it is preferable to provide it, because the permissions used to allow the modification of the reality data are determined from the iTwin.

With no iTwin specified, it may be possible to modify the reality data (e.g. the caller is the owner), but more permissions may be granted when the `iTwinId` is provided.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `realitydata_manage` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

example.displayName: Name of the reality data to modify
example.termsOfUse: Terms of use of reality data

realityData.termsOfUse: Terms of use of reality data

get.description:
---

Retrieves the metadata of a [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata).

The `iTwinId` parameter is optional, but it is preferable to provide it, because the permissions used to access the collection of reality data are determined from the iTwin. With no iTwin specified, the desired reality data can be retrieved (e.g. the caller is the owner), but it's more likely to get the expected result if you provide the `iTwinId`.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `realitydata_use` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

realityData.termsOfUse: Terms of use of reality data

delete.description:
---

Deletes a [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata).

To delete a reality data, it must be associated to only one (or zero) iTwin. If it has more associated iTwins, first [dissociate](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/reality-management/operations/dissociate-from-itwin/) the iTwins.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `realitydata_manage` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

404.description: This response indicates that the specified reality data was not found.

error.message: Requested reality data is not available.

post.summary: Associate a reality data to a iTwin
post.description:
---

Associates a [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata) to a iTwin.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `realitydata_create , realitydata_assign` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

error.message: Requested reality data is not available.

get.summary: Get all iTwins to which a reality data is associated
get.description:
---

Retrieves the list of iTwins associated to the specified [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata).

When no iTwin is associated to the reality data, an empty list is returned.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `realitydata_use` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

403.description: This response indicates that user does not have required permissions to get iTwins for a reality data.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

404.description: This response indicates that the requested reality data was not found.

error.message: Requested reality data is not available.

422.description: Invalid request to get iTwins for reality data. Please ensure that the request is valid.

error.message: Invalid RealityData request.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

patch.description:
---

Move a [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata) to a specified iTwin. A request body with the `iTwinId` parameter is required.

To successfully move a reality data :

- The destination iTwin must be in the same organization as the reality data.
- The destination iTwin must be in the same data center location as the reality data.
- The reality data must not already be associated to more than one iTwin.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization
User must have the `realitydata_manage` permission assigned at the iTwin level of both iTwins or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifier of the reality data. This identifier is assigned by the service at the creation of the reality data. It is also unique.

204.description: This response indicates that the reality data was successfully moved to the desired iTwin.

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

403.description: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

404.description: This response indicates that the reality data id or the iTwin id was not found.

error.message: Requested reality data is not available.

409.description: This response indicates that the reality data is already within the given iTwin.

error.message: Reality data is already within the given iTwin.

422.description: Invalid request to move reality data to iTwin. Please ensure that the request is valid.

error.message: Invalid RealityData request.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

get.summary: Retrieve write access to reality data container
get.description:
---
Retrieves the container's link with write access for an instance of [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata).

A container is where the reality data itself (a point cloud, a mesh, a collection of images, ...) is stored.

The container's link will allow you to upload/download data directly to this container.

A [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata) is always associated to exactly one container.

The `iTwinId` parameter is optional, but it is preferable to provide it, because the permissions used to access the container are determined from the iTwin. With no iTwin specified, the operation can succeed in some cases (e.g. the user is the reality data's owner), but we recommend to provide a `iTwinId`.

In future API versions, containers could be hosted in in different cloud providers, but for now, only Azure cloud is supported.

To store or retrieve data from a container, refer to the [Azure Blob Storage documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/).

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization
User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin or have realitydata_manage permission for write access assigned at the iTwin level.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

403.description: This response indicates that user does not have required permissions.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

404.description: This response indicates that the reality data not found.

error.message: Requested reality data is not available.

422.description: Invalid request to get access to reality data. Please ensure that the request is valid.

error.message: Invalid RealityData request.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

get.summary: Retrieve read access to reality data container
get.description:
---
Retrieves the container's link with read access for an instance of [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata).

A container is where the reality data itself (a point cloud, a mesh, a collection of images, ...) is stored.

The container's link will allow you to upload/download data directly to this container.

A [reality data](https://www.itwinjs.org/learning/glossary/#realitydata) is always associated to exactly one container.

The `iTwinId` parameter is optional, but it is preferable to provide it, because the permissions used to access the container are determined from the iTwin. With no iTwin specified, the operation can succeed in some cases (e.g. the user is the reality data's owner), but we recommend to provide a `iTwinId`.

In future API versions, containers could be hosted in in different cloud providers, but for now, only Azure cloud is supported.

To store or retrieve data from a container, refer to the [Azure Blob Storage documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/).

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization
User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin or have realitydata_use permission for read access assigned at the iTwin level.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

### Rate limits

All iTwin Platform API operations have a rate limit. For more documentation on that visit [Rate limits and quotas](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/rate-limits/) page.

---

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

403.description: This response indicates that user does not have required permissions.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

404.description: This response indicates that the reality data not found.

error.message: Requested reality data is not available.

422.description: Invalid request to get access to reality data.. Please ensure that the request is valid.

error.message: Invalid RealityData request.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

startDateTime.description: ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC) that indicates when the data acquisition started. E.g. '2017-05-10T13:43:03Z'

endDateTime.description: ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC) that indicates when the data acquisition ended. E.g. '2017-05-10T13:43:03Z'

extent.title: Extent
extent.description: Extent of a reality data, delimited by southwest and northeast coordinates.

coordinate.title: Coordinate

latitude.description: Latitude. Latitude ranges between -90 and 90 degrees, inclusive.

longitude.description: Longitude. Longitude ranges between -180 and 180 degrees, inclusive.

coordinate.description: Coordinate used to define an extent.

crs.title: Crs

id.description: Identifier of the coordinate reference system.

verticalId.description: Optional identifier of the vertical coordinate reference system.

crs.description: Details about the reality data's coordinate reference system.

iTwin-association-response.title: iTwin Association Response
iTwin-association-response.description: Ids of associated iTwin and reality data.

iTwin-association.title: iTwin Association
iTwin-association.description: Defines an association between a reality data and a iTwin

type.description: Type of container. Currently, only 'AzureBlobSasUrl' is supported.

access.description: Type of access user have to container.

container-details.title: Container Details

type.description: Type of container. Currently, only 'AzureBlobSasUrl' is supported.

container-details.description: Provides a link and other details for a reality data container

id.description: Identifier of the reality data. This identifier is assigned by the service at the creation of the reality data. It is also unique.

displayName.description: The name of the reality data. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

type.description: A key indicating the format of the data. The type property should be a specific indication of the format of the reality data. Given a type, the consuming software should be able to determine if it has the capacity to open the reality data. Although the type field is a free string some specific values are reserved and other values should be selected judiciously. Look at the documentation for an exhaustive list of reserved [reality data types](/apis/reality-management/rm-rd-details/#types).

next-page-link.title: Next Page Link
next-page-link.description: URL for getting the next page of data, if applicable.

request-body-create.title: Reality Data

classification.description: Specific value constrained field that indicates the nature of the reality data. Allowed values: 'Terrain', 'Imagery', 'Pinned', 'Model', 'Undefined'

type.description: A key indicating the format of the data. Valid values include '3MX', 'PointCloud', RealityMesh3DTiles', ... See documentation for an exhaustive list of registered [reality data types](/apis/reality-management/rm-rd-details/#types).

tags.description: A string of lower case characters. Upper case characters will be accepted as input but will be transferred to lower case. Limits: 60 characters. 15 tags per reality data. Prohibited characters: \ / : * ? , " < > |

dataset.description: This field can be used to define a loose grouping of reality data. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

group.description: The group can be used to define a second level of grouping. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

attribution.description: The attribution of the reality data.

termsOfUse.description: Terms of use of the reality data.

rootDocument.description: Used to indicate the root document of the reality data.

authoring.description: A boolean value that is true if the data is being created. It is false if the data has been completely uploaded.

ownerId.description: Identifier of the owner of the reality data.

request-body-create.description: Request body for reality data creation.

id.description: Identifier of the reality data. This identifier is assigned by the service at the creation of the reality data. It is also unique.

displayName.description: The name of the reality data. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

dataset.description: This field can be used to define a loose grouping of reality data. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

group.description: The group can be used to define a second level of grouping. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

description.description: A textual description of the reality data. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

attribution.description: The attribution of the reality data.

termsOfUse.description: Terms of use of the reality data.

tags.description: A string of lower case characters. Upper case characters will be accepted as input but will be transferred to lower case. Limits: 60 characters. 15 tags per reality data. Prohibited characters: \ / : * ? , " < > |

rootDocument.description: Used to indicate the root document of the reality data. The root document can be in a subfolder and is then specified as “Tile_Root.json” or “Folder1/SubFolder1/File.json” for example.

size.description: The size of the reality data in Kilobytes.

classification.description: Specific value constrained field that indicates the nature of the reality data. The following values are currently supported: Terrain, Imagery, Pinned, Model, Undefined.

type.description: A key indicating the format of the data. The type property should be a specific indication of the format of the reality data. Given a type, the consuming software should be able to determine if it has the capacity to open the reality data. Although the type field is a free string some specific values are reserved and other values should be selected judiciously. Look at the documentation for an exhaustive list of reserved [reality data types](/apis/reality-management/rm-rd-details/#types).

authoring.description: A boolean value that is true if the data is being created. It is false if the data has been completely uploaded.

dataCenterLocation.description: Identifies the data center location used to store the reality data.

modifiedDateTime.description: ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC) of last modification of the reality data. E.g. '2017–05–10T13:43:03Z'

lastAccessedDateTime.description: ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC) of last access of the reality data. E.g. '2017–05–10T13:43:03Z'

createdDateTime.description: ISO-8601 compliant time (UTC) of the creation of the reality data. E.g. '2017–05–10T13:43:03Z'

ownerId.description: Identifier of the owner of the reality data.

tags.description: A string of lower case characters. Upper case characters will be accepted as input but will be transferred to lower case. Limits: 60 characters. 15 tags per reality data. Prohibited characters: \ / : * ? , " < > |

group.description: The group can be used to define a second level of grouping. This property may not contain any control sequence such as a URL or code.

termsOfUse.description: Terms of use of the reality data.

rootDocument.description: Used to indicate the root document of the reality data.

authoring.description: A boolean value that is true if the data is being created. It is false if the data has been completely uploaded.

reality-data-iTwins-response.title: List of iTwins Associated to a Reality Data
reality-data-iTwins-response.description: List of all iTwins associated to a reality data.

iTwinId.description: Identifier of the destination iTwin to move the reality data to.

code.description: One of a server-defined set of error codes.

message.description: A human-readable representation of the error.

details.description: Optional array of more specific errors.

DetailedError.description: Contains error information and an array of more specific errors.

DetailedErrorResponse.title: Detailed Error Response
DetailedErrorResponse.description: Gives details for an error that occurred while handling the request. Note that clients MUST NOT assume that every failed request will produce an object of this schema, or that all of the properties in the response will be non-null, as the error may have prevented this response from being constructed.

code.description: One of a server-defined set of error codes.

message.description: A human-readable representation of the error.

ErrorResponse.title: Error Response
ErrorResponse.description: Gives details for an error that occurred while handling the request. Note that clients MUST NOT assume that every failed request will produce an object of this schema, or that all of the properties in the response will be non-null, as the error may have prevented this response from being constructed.
